title: Today's Tech Brief: Cisco's AI Experiment with Security Incident Reports
summary: Cisco used AI to write security incident reports with mixed results, requiring detailed prompts and still producing errors.
imageAlt: Image from source article for Cisco used AI to write security incident reports, with mixed results
imageCaption: Cisco used AI to write security incident reports, with mixed results
imageSourceTitle: Cisco used AI to write security incident reports, with mixed results

body.text: I'm checking out a recent report from The Register about Cisco's attempt to use AI to write security incident reports. Apparently, they had mixed results. According to the article, Cisco found that generating solid output required a lot of detailed prompts - and even then, the AI produced errors and typos. It's a bit like trying to get a recipe from a friend who's still learning to cook - you get the gist, but it's missing that special something.

What stands out to me is that AI-generated reports need a lot of specific guidance to be useful. It's not just a matter of plugging in some data and getting a polished report. The AI needs detailed prompts to produce something that's even close to accurate. And even with all that prompting, the output can still have mistakes. I guess that's what happens when you're trying to automate something that usually requires a human touch.
More News: What's Happening in Tech

In other news, I've been reading about the latest developments in the world of tech. It seems like Google is making some big strides in quantum computing, and there's a new startup that's trying to make virtual reality more accessible. It's always exciting to see what's on the horizon, and I'm looking forward to seeing how these advancements play out.

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.
